libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: For tests that exercise com.mchange.v2.naming.JavaBeanReferenceMaker.createReference(...) or JavaBeanObjectFactory.getObjectInstance(...), set and restore the system property com.mchange.v2.naming.referenceableJavaBeanClassWhitelist to the fully qualified generated test bean class name(s). For ReferenceableUtils.referenceToObject(...) overloads that do not pass an explicit allowedFactoryClassNames Set, set and restore com.mchange.v2.naming.objectFactoryWhitelist to the test ObjectFactory class name(s), or prefer the overload that passes an explicit Set. Avoid using '*' unless intentionally testing wildcard behavior, and restore/clear properties after each test to avoid whitelist intersection surprises.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8716 [Automation] java run fails for com.mchange:mchange-commons-java:0.6.0; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: If both system properties and a PropertiesConfig define the same whitelist, mchange-commons-java uses the intersection, so mismatched values can still reject otherwise valid test classes.

The artifact declares optional integrations for Typesafe Config and logging backends, but they are not required for the existing scaffold-style coverage and should not be added unless tests deliberately cover those optional paths.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: mchange-commons-java 0.6.0 adds hard security gates around JNDI JavaBean/reference dereferencing; meaningful tests of JavaBeanReferenceMaker, JavaBeanObjectFactory, or ReferenceableUtils.referenceToObject need explicit whitelists set in test code.
